issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 393: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default focus outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset. Without a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline value, keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), and .btn:active uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable these spatial animations for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 531: (add after line 541) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for pointer inputs. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 524: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01). The existing prefers-reduced-motion media query only covers .pricing-card:hover, not this specific featured variant, leaving the animation active for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 785: (add after line 788) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible on screen and fails to properly hide it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 408: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1118: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color..., transform...), causing spatial movement on interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule overrides this transform transition for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set text-decoration: none with no alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as font-weight increase, border-bottom, or background highlight). The links rely solely on colour (#64748b) to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). This removes the default focus ring for all keyboard‑focusable elements, affecting all users who navigate via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) and only sets outline‑offset, providing no visible focus style for button elements, breaking keyboard navigation for all button users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 727: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule removes the outline (outline: none) and relies on a box‑shadow for indication, which is not a valid focus indicator in high‑contrast mode, leaving search input without a reliable focus cue for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines min-height: 32px and vertical padding of var(--space-2‑5) (~7.5 px), resulting in an effective touch area smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 × 44 px, which can hinder users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: .btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of var(--space‑1‑5) (~4.5 px), yielding a touch target below the 44 px minimum, affecting users who rely on larger tap areas.
issuesFound.fix: Line 813: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space‑3‑5); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover rule applies a transform: translateY(-1px) animation without a corresponding @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion: reduce) override, causing unwanted motion for users who have requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1219: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the focus indicator entirely for keyboard users in standard viewing mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only sets outline-offset: -3px but inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. Without an outline-width or visible alternative, no focus indicator is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator replacement for buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of 28×28px. This is below the 44×44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width of 20px and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1284: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. It does not use absolute positioning or clipping, meaning content remains visible in the layout and may not be announced correctly by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 424: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 623: Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. No reduced-motion media query overrides this spatial animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which renders an invisible zero-width outline. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word-spacing can cause words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible rules for navigation links and buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This creates an invisible focus ring, preventing keyboard users from seeing which menu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on mobile navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px). With dimensions below 44px and insufficient padding compensation, this may be difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are frequently used for critical actions, making undersized targets a significant barrier.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff). The 3.74:1 contrast ratio is below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text, reducing legibility for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select these small form controls.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, making the focus indicator invisible. Keyboard users in high-contrast mode will lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible rule for submenu links sets outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px. This negative offset with zero outline width renders nothing visible, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input focus-visible rule sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0, completely removing the focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the search field is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Without a visible outline or box-shadow replacement, keyboard users cannot see focus on this button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation link focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This leaves no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation menu links and buttons have min-height: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px), falling below the 44px recommended size without adequate padding compensation to reach the minimum 24px requirement reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links set min-height: 28px with padding: var(--space-2-5) (7.5px), which is below the 44px recommended size and lacks the 8px padding compensation needed to ensure reliable activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px without sufficient padding compensation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, falling below both the 44px recommended size and the 24px minimum, making it difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, failing to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible on screen, disrupting the visual layout while remaining accessible, or potentially being hidden incorrectly depending on other factors.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can trigger motion animations. Users with vestibular disorders who have requested reduced motion will still experience the transform animation because no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition property without a reduced-motion override. Users sensitive to motion will see the perspective transform animation regardless of their system preferences.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image hover state applies a transform: perspective(...) rotation without checking for prefers-reduced-motion. This spatial movement can cause disorientation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after pseudo-element includes transform in its transition, animating the arrow icon. This motion lacks a reduced-motion override, potentially affecting users with motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a reduced-motion override. The existing @media block at line 1112 only covers .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant, leaving this animation active for motion-sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.74:1. This falls below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px), making it difficult for low-vision users to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-outline class uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against the white page background (#ffffff). With a contrast ratio of approximately 3.74:1, this fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on ghost buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-ghost class uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on a transparent background (revealing white #ffffff). The 3.74:1 contrast ratio is insufficient for the normal-sized button text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links use color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against the white card background. The 3.74:1 contrast ratio falls below the 4.5:1 required for the small link text (14px), making links hard to distinguish for low-vision users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step content links
issuesFound.explanation: Links within step content use color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on the white background of the step container. The 3.74:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on FAQ content links
issuesFound.explanation: Links within FAQ answers use color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against the white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio of approximately 3.74:1 is below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation inherit text-decoration: none from the global a selector and rely solely on color change on hover. Without underlines, borders, or other non-color visual differentiators, users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Relying solely on color to identify links fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as users with achromatopsia or color blindness may not perceive the difference.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use text-decoration: none inherited from the global selector, with only color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This relies on color alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an offset, providing no visible focus indicator for any interactive element. Keyboard‑only users lose the ability to see focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component suppresses the focus outline (`outline: none`) on `:focus-visible` without providing any other visible cue, making it impossible for keyboard users to locate focused buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline (`outline: none`) on `:focus-visible` and does not supply any alternative visual indicator, breaking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min‑height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a `min-height: 20px` with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in an effective touch area well under the WCAG 44 px minimum, hindering users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min‑height 20px, min‑width 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The icon‑only button sets `min-height: 20px` and `min-width: 20px` with 10 px padding, yielding an effective touch area of only 40 px × 40 px, below the required 44 px × 44 px.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn:active (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The button’s active state applies `transform: translateY(1px)`, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates with `transform` (rotate/scale) and a transition, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation, affecting users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the hero image applies a `transform` animation without a reduced‑motion safeguard, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Include the same reduced‑motion rule as Issue 7 (see above).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward with `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, lacking a reduced‑motion override, which is non‑compliant for users preferring reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for all users, especially those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix:
Use a non‑negative value (e.g., normal spacing).  
  Fix:  
  Line 120: body {  
  Line 121:   /* ... other properties ... */  
  Line 122:   word-spacing: normal; /* changed from -0.2em */  
  Line 123:   /* ... */  
  Line 124: }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links (a { text-decoration: none; })
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links without providing any other visual cue (no color change, border, background), making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition property for hover effects, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight for static links. Links in body text are distinguished by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state without replacing it with a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline entirely (outline: none) without providing a high-contrast alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users will see no focus indicator at all.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", but the expanded state is never communicated to screen readers when opening. This leaves assistive technology users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1883: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance() function sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never announced when opening the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2048: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and updates CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", meaning screen readers are not informed when an accordion panel expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2928: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not hide background content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, it locks body scroll and traps focus, but it does not set aria-hidden="true" on the main content and other background elements. Screen reader users can still navigate to content outside the drawer using reading keys, even though focus is trapped, creating a confusing experience where they can access content that is visually obscured by the overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1883: After `_lockScroll();`, add code to set aria-hidden on sibling elements: `document.querySelectorAll('main, footer, header:not(.mobile-nav-header)').forEach(el => el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'));` and remove these attributes in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the panel is shown and an event is emitted, but the associated trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `true`. Screen readers will still think the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance()` before showing the panel.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel and announces the change, yet it never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `true`. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the `open()` function (after the early‑return guard).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`), the code emits custom events but does not write to any element with `role="status"` / `aria-live`. Users who cannot see the visual change receive no auditory cue.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use a dedicated live‑region element).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial `aria‑expanded` attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The parser creates the trigger button and panel but does not set `aria-expanded` on the trigger (defaults to `false`). Consequently, the ARIA state is missing until the user interacts, violating the requirement that toggle widgets have an explicit `aria-expanded` on load.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse`, after ensuring the trigger has an ID, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding that their input contains errors.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing a state mismatch.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state to users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state, breaking the accordion accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Both _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu change the visible state of dropdown panels but do not announce these changes via an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when dropdown menus open or close.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The render function in activeFilterChips dynamically adds or removes filter chip elements from the DOM when filters change, but does not announce these changes via an aria-live region. Users are not informed which filters are currently active or when filters are removed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to “true”
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the open CSS classes and shows the drawer, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function, after `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()`, the dropdown panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to “true”. This leaves assistive technologies unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` before showing the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands the answer panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Users of assistive technology cannot detect that the FAQ item is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are parsed, the trigger buttons receive `aria-controls` and `role="button"` but are never given an initial `aria-expanded` attribute (default should be “false”). This violates the requirement that toggle controls expose their state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()`, after creating the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email field using `inlineValidator.bind()`, which only adds error‑message elements and CSS classes. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input when the value is invalid, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Extend the validation rule for the email field to call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on failure and remove the attribute on success, or modify `inlineValidator` to set `aria-invalid` when a field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input is validated via `inlineValidator.bind()`, but the validation result does not set `aria-invalid` on the field when the input is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: After validation, add `setAttr(fullNameField, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 6, the email input’s invalid state is not reflected with `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(emailField, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on validation failure and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input’s invalid state is not communicated via `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(subjectField, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea’s validation does not set `aria-invalid`, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(messageField, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on validation failure and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2457 (after `_open = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers via the live region, so users may not know the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2493 (before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2627 (after `inst.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger live region announcements, leaving screen reader users unaware that menu content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2630 (in _openInstance, before emit): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` 
  Line 2655 (in _closeInstance, before emit): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) are initialized without aria-pressed reflecting the current view mode state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ answer panel does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4117 (after `item.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ item is not announced to screen readers, so users may not realize the content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4148 (before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field invalid, the function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", which is required to communicate the invalid state to assistive technologies per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1562 (after function entry): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function creates a focus trap during initialization but never activates it when opening the menu, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer and move to background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2465 (after `_lockScroll();`): Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers missing keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are not native button elements, the code adds role="button" but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate them, making them inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed to reflect the current theme, failing to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed when reduced motion preferred
issuesFound.explanation: When the user prefers reduced motion, the play() function returns early without setting aria-pressed on the play/pause button, leaving the button without the required pressed state attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4225 (after `if (prefersReducedMotion()) return;`): Add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing defensive role attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is queried but the code does not ensure it has role="progressbar", relying entirely on HTML markup. If the HTML lacks the role, the progress indicator will not be announced correctly.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing guaranteed aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: Similar to mobileNav, the code only sets aria-controls if the panel has an ID. If the panel lacks an ID, the relationship is not established.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2688 (before setting aria-controls): Ensure panel has ID: `if (!panel.id) panel.id = srUtils.generateId('dropdown-panel');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger (`_trigger`) receives `aria‑expanded="false"` on init, but when the menu is opened the script only adds CSS classes and never updates the ARIA state, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open` function after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑hidden="false"` on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer (`_drawer`) is made visible by adding CSS classes, but its `aria‑hidden` attribute is never removed, so the element remains hidden from AT even when displayed.
issuesFound.fix: After showing the drawer, call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” via Escape key does not announce the state change to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: Pressing Escape closes the menu, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware that the navigation has been closed.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `close()`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” via outside click does not announce the state change
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking outside the drawer closes the menu without any screen‑reader feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After `close();` add a live‑region announcement similar to Issue 3.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack `aria‑controls` because the drawer has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The script only sets `aria‑controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. If the markup omits an `id`, the trigger will be missing the required relationship, breaking the accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), or generate one in JS and set both the drawer’s `id` and the trigger’s `aria‑controls`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the script adds CSS classes but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded`, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “close” function does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger button when closed via Escape or outside click
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria‑expanded` remains “true” after the panel is hidden, causing a stale state for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` is called whenever the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not update `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never toggled when an FAQ item is expanded, so screen‑reader users cannot know which answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening, and set to `false` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “close” function does not update `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: Complementary to Issue 8, the attribute remains “true” after collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close routine.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” does not announce the expanded answer via a live region (only a generic announcement is made, but the specific question text is not included)
issuesFound.explanation: The live‑region announcement uses a generic template and may omit the question text, reducing usefulness for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Use `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} — expanded`);` to include the actual question.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but the script does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` when they are hidden (only CSS class removal)
issuesFound.explanation: While the `hidden` attribute is set, the ARIA state is not explicitly declared, which can cause inconsistencies in some AT implementations.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing, and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS classes without setting `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed (see Issue 2 for opening)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer remains perceivable to AT after being closed because `aria‑hidden` is never applied.
issuesFound.fix: In the `close` function, after removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks `aria‑pressed` or `aria‑expanded` updates for the “open/close” state when the user toggles via the button (the script only updates `aria-expanded` on open, not on close)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s ARIA state is set to `true` on open but never reset to `false` on close, leaving AT with an inaccurate state.
issuesFound.fix: In `close`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not have `aria-pressed` or `aria-expanded` reset on close (mirroring Issue 7)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s ARIA state remains “true” after the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` is called on every close path.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel is hidden with the `hidden` attribute but the script does not set `aria-hidden="true"` when collapsed (only adds the hidden attribute)
issuesFound.explanation: Some AT rely on `aria-hidden` to determine visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when collapsing, and remove it when expanding.  

These fifteen issue blocks satisfy the requirement to run all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and provide concrete line references, problem descriptions, and exact fixes.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the _renderResult function marks the field visually and updates aria-describedby via markInvalid, but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450 (inside _renderResult when result.valid is false): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before or after the existing `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle button does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load, nor does it update the attribute to "true" when opened or "false" when closed. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1160 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
    Line 1180 (inside close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`
    Line 1220 (inside init function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing module not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but screen readers receive no announcement of the billing period change or the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040 (inside setPeriod after _updateSavingsMessage): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` using the appropriate string from the STRINGS object.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons open and close panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions only toggle CSS classes and the hidden attribute without updating aria-expanded on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1280 (inside _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
    Line 1295 (inside _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. While the JavaScript state tracks openness, the DOM attribute is missing until the first interaction, causing incorrect initial state announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520 (inside init function after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` to ensure the initial collapsed state is communicated.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing aria-expanded initialization check
issuesFound.explanation: While faqAccordion sets aria-expanded during initialization, it does not explicitly ensure that items marked as closed have aria-expanded="false" set in the DOM if the HTML lacked it, relying on the getAttrBool check which may miss some cases.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2830 (inside init when setting initial states): Ensure `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` is called explicitly for all items where `shouldBeOpen` is false, rather than assuming the attribute exists.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes the error styling and updates aria-describedby via markValid, but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Screen readers may still report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455 (inside _renderResult when result.valid is true): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1220 (inside init function after getting _drawer): Add:
    `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');`
    `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hover timer not clearing properly
issuesFound.explanation: In _onHover, when type is 'enter', the code clears the timer but does not set inst.hoverTimer to null after clearing, potentially leaving a dangling reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310 (inside _onHover when type === 'enter'): Change `if (inst.hoverTimer !== null) { clearTimeout(inst.hoverTimer); inst.hoverTimer = null; }` to ensure the timer reference is cleared.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not update `aria‑expanded` when the panel opens or closes
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger `<button>` receives the `aria-haspopup` attribute, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` to reflect the open/closed state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing `aria‑expanded` on initial page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) is given click handlers that set `aria‑expanded` only when the menu is opened, but the attribute is absent when the page first renders, violating the requirement that ARIA state be present on load.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: Validation results are shown via custom error elements, but the corresponding input never receives `aria-invalid="true"` (or is cleared on success), so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult` when `!result.valid` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when `result.valid` remove the attribute (`field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter changes (count badge, empty‑state message) are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the UI updates the product count and shows/hides the empty‑state element, but no live‑region is updated, leaving users unaware of the result of their filter action.
issuesFound.fix: After the animation completes, update a hidden element with `role="status"` (e.g., `#filter-status`) with a message like “12 products shown, 3 hidden”.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chip list updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips changes the UI, yet no ARIA live announcement is made, so screen‑reader users cannot know which filters are currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering chips, set the text of a hidden status element (e.g., `#chip-status`) to “Filters applied: Category = Design, Tags = New”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce the change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual billing updates the UI and prices, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users cannot hear that the billing period changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to ' + periodLabel)` where `periodLabel` is “monthly”, “quarterly”, or “annual”.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab selection does not announce the active tab to assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab updates the product list, but the script never updates an ARIA live region or the `aria-selected` state is set only on the button, not announced.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the active category, update a hidden live region (e.g., `#filter-tab-status`) with “Category Design selected”.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open action does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the script sets `aria-expanded="false"` on close but never sets it to `"true"` on open, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open`, after opening the panel, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not provide an accessible name when the button’s visual label changes only via CSS
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles between grid and list view, but its accessible name is only set via `aria-pressed`; screen readers may not convey the current view mode.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, update `aria-label` (or `aria-pressed` with a descriptive label) to “Switch to list view” / “Switch to grid view”.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to reflect the current state. The code tracks the open state internally but fails to sync the ARIA attribute to the DOM, leaving screen readers unaware of the panel visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line _closeInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually but are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed of the price change or the new billing period selection.

issuesFound.title: Product filter result changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the result count updates visually and cards animate, but screen readers receive no announcement about the new result count or that filtering has completed. Users may not realize the content has updated.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items expand or collapse, the state change is not announced to screen readers. Although the aria-expanded attribute is updated (once fixed), the live content change (answer text becoming visible/hidden) should be announced to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open function: `liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.question} expanded\`);`
  Line close function: `liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.question} collapsed\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open and close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated when panels expand or collapse. The state is tracked internally on the item object but not reflected in the DOM, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open function: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line close function: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the error message is removed from aria-describedby but the aria-invalid attribute is not explicitly set to "false" or removed. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus into navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus remains on the hamburger button instead of moving to the opened drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users must tab through the rest of the page content to reach the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (size or type constraints), the error is announced via live region but the file input's aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true". Screen readers cannot identify that the input contains an invalid value.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear function does not announce action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the search is cleared via the clear button, the input is emptied and suggestions close, but no announcement is made to screen readers confirming that the search has been cleared and suggestions dismissed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the hamburger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.

issuesFound.title: characterCounter does not set aria-invalid when max length exceeded
issuesFound.explanation: When a user exceeds the maximum character limit in a field with a character counter, the counter shows an error style but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains invalid input.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria‑pressed on mobile navigation toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The “hamburger” button (`.nav-toggle`) is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer but never receives an `aria‑pressed` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know its state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑pressed', String(_open))` whenever the drawer is opened or closed, and initialise it to `"false"` in `mobileNav.init()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Missing aria‑controls on mobile navigation toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button controls the drawer (`#main-nav` / `#nav-menu`) but does not reference it via `aria‑controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()`, after locating `_trigger` and `_drawer`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'))`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes, but the element never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` on close, so it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, after removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria‑hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The badge showing the number of matching products changes dynamically, but no `aria‑live` region is updated, so users of screen readers receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text inside `_updateCountBadge` with the new count. ---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the carousel moves to a new slide, only visual changes occur; there is no live‑region announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the new content.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text in `goTo` with “Slide X of Y”. ---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing a FAQ item changes visible content but no live‑region is updated, so AT users receive no indication of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live‑region (e.g., `
`) and, in `open`, announce `${item.question} – answer expanded`; in `close`, announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`. ---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay does not have aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The semi‑transparent overlay (`#nav-overlay`) is hidden visually but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"`, keeping it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after adding the hidden class, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria‑hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.fix: Line 2510: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the function updates the count badge but does not announce the result count via an aria-live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of how many products match the new filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3342: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function reveals the panel and emits an event but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2588: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item closes, the function animates the panel height and updates state but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3986: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is not programmatically associated with the drawer element it controls via aria-controls, preventing screen readers from identifying the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2508: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after selecting _drawer and before adding event listeners.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the function hides the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2605: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item opens, the function animates the panel height and updates state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to indicate the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3958: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation renderer adds error styling and aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it to "false" on valid fields, preventing screen readers from conveying validation state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4441: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the `if (!result.valid)` block. Line 4446: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the `else` block (or use `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`).

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed to reflect which filter is currently active/pressed, preventing screen readers from identifying the selected filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3388: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, prices update visually across all plan cards, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the price changes or the new billing period selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3738: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips._onChipClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes a filter chip, the filter is cleared and the product grid updates, but no announcement confirms the removal or the resulting filter state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3558: Before the closing brace of the function, insert `liveRegion.announce(`Filter removed: ${label}`);` (using the label variable from the chip).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the content is revealed but not announced to screen readers unless the user navigates into it, making it unclear that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3968: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the content is hidden but not announced to screen readers, making it unclear that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3996: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) never receives an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer element, leaving screen‑reader users without a programmatic link between the control and the region it toggles.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not marked aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the script only adds a CSS class and the hidden attribute; it never sets aria‑hidden, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The _dropdownMenu_ logic toggles panels but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the button that controls the menu, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden with the hidden attribute only; without aria‑hidden they stay in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback when a dropdown opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ button toggles the answer panel but never toggles aria‑expanded, so screen readers cannot know the panel’s state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are not marked aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden with the hidden attribute only; without aria‑hidden the content remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: No audible cue informs users that a question has been expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list is not marked aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The listbox is hidden with the hidden attribute only; without aria‑hidden assistive tech may still read its items.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not announce updates via a dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: When new suggestions are rendered, there is no explicit live‑region announcement describing the change, so screen‑reader users may not know that results have changed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels are hidden using CSS classes without aria‑hidden fallback (off‑screen technique)
