issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens a dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function dynamically filters product cards and updates the visible count, but does not announce the result count or filter changes via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing filtered results\`);` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes a dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The trigger continues to report an expanded state to assistive technologies even when the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer.mount missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but omits the required aria-expanded="false" attribute for the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line suggestionListRenderer.mount: Insert `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown instance, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger, but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false". The attribute must be present on page load to reflect the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the hamburger trigger button (_trigger). Users cannot perceive that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization function obtains the trigger element (_trigger) but does not set aria-expanded="false" to reflect the initial closed state of the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;` (after the null check for _trigger and _drawer)

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the search suggestions panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element (_inputEl). Screen readers cannot convey that the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch._open: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the suggestion panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element, violating the requirement that the state reflect the visibility of the controlled listbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch._close: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `if (!_searchState.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid management
issuesFound.explanation: The function renders validation results by adding CSS classes and updating error messages, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`, Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function links an error element to a field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, which is required to communicate the error state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving the field marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the function before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable and documents that it activates a focus trap when open, but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Keyboard users can tab out of the open drawer and lose focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer, returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();`, Line mobileNav.init: Insert `if (_drawer) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer, returnFocus: _trigger });` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function changes the visibility of the mobile drawer but never sets or updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` (or use `setExpanded`) inside `open()` and `close()` so the attribute reflects the current state.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick` the trigger’s visibility is toggled, but the button never receives an `aria-expanded` update. Without this ARIA state, assistive technologies cannot convey the open/closed status of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and after closing call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Period toggle buttons lack `aria‑controls` linking to the price panel
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons control the price display inside each pricing card, yet no `aria-controls` attribute is set on the buttons to reference the corresponding panel’s `id`. This breaks the required relationship between the control and the region it updates.
issuesFound.fix:
When initializing each button, generate a unique id for the price container (e.g., `price-${plan.id}`) and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', generatedId);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack `aria‑controls`/`aria‑describedby`
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s “Previous”, “Next”, and “Submit” buttons control the visibility of step panels but do not expose a relationship via `aria-controls` (or `aria-describedby`). Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the panel it shows.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign each button an `aria-controls` attribute that references the `id` of the target step panel (`setAttr(prevBtn, 'aria-controls', stepId)` etc.).

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not expose status via an ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` creates a status element (`_statusEl`) but never assigns `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` to it. Users are not notified when files are added, rejected, or uploaded.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `_statusEl`, call `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status'); setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Media player controller does not expose caption toggle via `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The caption toggle (pressing “c”) shows/hides the first `textTrack`, but the button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute referencing the caption track element, nor does it expose the current state via `aria-pressed`. Screen‑reader users cannot discover or control captions.
issuesFound.fix:
When binding a media element, add a caption‑toggle button (or use the media element itself) with `setAttr(media, 'aria-controls', track.id); setAttr(media, 'aria-pressed', String(track.mode==='showing'));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons lack `aria-pressed` to indicate current theme
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle elements (`[data-theme-toggle]`) change the site theme but do not convey the active theme to assistive technology via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: In `themeSwitcher.init()` after setting the theme, update each button: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle')===_current));`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and linking error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input elements, preventing screen readers from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5435: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`
  Line 5440: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Additionally, the init() function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" or set aria-controls on the trigger. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2140: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
  Line 2141: Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);` (if not already present)
  Line 2180: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
  Line 2215: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` and `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions control dropdown panel visibility but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing when the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2255: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 2270: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing active/inactive states) but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as toggle buttons but the _syncToggleState function does not set aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the faqAccordion module animate the FAQ panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the expanded/collapsed state of the accordion items.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5055: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`
  Line 5080: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen readers about the price change or the new billing period selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4460: Add after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    period === 'year' 
      ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing'
      : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls set to reference the drawer element's ID, failing to establish the programmatic relationship required by the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils only links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description association but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after the existing code in markValid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus to drawer content
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the first focusable element within the drawer, violating WCAG focus management requirements for modal-like interfaces.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via an aria-live region, leaving blind users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3800: Add after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    (STRINGS.filterResults || 'Showing {count} products')
      .replace('{count}', String(totalMatches))
      .replace('{category}', _productFilterState.activeCategory)
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion opens and closes panels without announcing the state change to screen readers via the live region, leaving users unaware that content has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5060: Add after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : item.question + ' expanded');
  ```
  Line 5095: Add after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : item.question + ' collapsed');
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2260: Add after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
  ```
  Line 2285: Add after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdowns, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false", leaving the initial state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer, but the code never sets or updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger, so screen‑reader users cannot know the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened the panel is shown, but the corresponding trigger button never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value, leaving the accessibility tree out‑of‑sync.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance`, after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; similarly ensure the close function sets it to `"false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a two‑state control (open/closed) but never updates `aria-pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons to convey state to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; after closing set it to `"false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) changes the pricing display but does not update `aria-pressed` on the button, so its state is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick` (or in `setPeriod`) call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (inline validator)
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so AT does not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Validation success does not clear aria‑invalid (inline validator)
issuesFound.explanation: On successful validation the code removes the error message but does not clear `aria-invalid`, leaving the input incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
When `result.valid` call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`).

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile‑nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden on load, yet the trigger lacks the required ARIA state, violating rule #3.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` (and optionally `aria-controls`).

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdowns are closed by default; the trigger must start with `aria-expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register`, after setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for FAQ triggers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are collapsed initially; the trigger must have `aria-expanded="false"` so AT knows the default state.
issuesFound.fix: In `parse`, after setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself, so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1953 (after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled by adding/removing CSS classes, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never updated to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2205 (in `open` function, after `_lockScroll();`): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2224 (in `close` function, after `_unlockScroll();`): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count updates visually but is not announced to screen readers, leaving blind users unaware that results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020 (after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown/hidden by removing/setting the `hidden` attribute and toggling classes, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` state is not updated programmatically when the dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2356 (in `_openInstance` after `requestAnimationFrame`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2370 (in `_closeInstance` before `emit`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) toggle active states but do not communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255 (inside `forEach` after `toggleClass`): `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion items are expanded/collapsed via `animateHeight` and class toggles, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is not set to `"true"` or `"false"` when the state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4158 (in `open` function, after `item.open = true;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 4183 (in `close` function, after `item.open = false;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Newly registered dropdown triggers are not initialised with `aria-expanded="false"`, so screen readers do not know the panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410 (after `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`): `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialised with `aria-expanded="false"` despite the suggestion list being hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2915 (after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is shown/hidden, but the combobox input element’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never updated to reflect whether the popup is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2855 (in `_open` function, after `_searchState.open = true;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2865 (in `_close` function, after `_searchState.open = false;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons function as a tablist but only toggle CSS classes; they do not set `aria-selected` to indicate which tab is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3058 (inside `forEach` after `toggleClass`): `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared/validated, the function removes the error description but does not reset `aria-invalid` to `"false"` or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly flagged as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1972 (after the if/else block handling aria-describedby): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with `aria-expanded="false"` on page load, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial DOM state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2282 (after `_initialised = true;`): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  Line 2283 (optional but recommended): `if (_drawer && _drawer.id) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers, so users do not receive confirmation that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2212 (in `open` before `emit`): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 2231 (in `close` before `emit`): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced, leaving screen reader users unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2358 (in `_openInstance` after `setAttr`): `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');`
  Line 2372 (in `_closeInstance` after `setAttr`): `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ answers is not announced to screen readers, even though visual feedback is provided.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4175 (in `open` function, before `emit`): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : \`\${item.question} expanded\`);`
  Line 4195 (in `close` function, before `emit`): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : \`\${item.question} collapsed\`);`

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap exports reference error breaks focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The `exports` object is referenced in `activate` and `deactivate` functions before it is defined (temporal dead zone), causing a ReferenceError that prevents the focus trap from activating, breaking keyboard accessibility for modals and drawers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1805 (after `let _observer = null;`): Add `const exports = {};`
  Line 1822 (in `activate`): Keep `emit(container, 'focustrap:activate', { trap: exports });` (now valid)
  Line 1850 (in `deactivate`): Keep `emit(container, 'focustrap:deactivate', { trap: exports });` (now valid)
  Line 1875 (end of function): Change `const exports = Object.freeze({ activate, deactivate, pause, resume, isActive });` to `Object.assign(exports, { activate, deactivate, pause, resume, isActive }); return Object.freeze(exports);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never receives `aria‑pressed`, violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance(inst)` shows a submenu by removing `hidden` and adding a CSS class, yet it never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen readers cannot know the submenu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance(inst)` hides the submenu but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` before or after hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not set `aria‑expanded` when suggestions open
issuesFound.explanation: `_open()` marks the suggestion list as visible but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the search `<input>`. Users of assistive tech cannot tell that a list of suggestions is available.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: During `siteSearch.init()` the input receives `aria-autocomplete`, `aria-controls`, etc., but no initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, leaving the state undefined on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the live‑region listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives `aria‑pressed`, so its on/off state is not exposed.
issuesFound.fix: In the click handler (`_onToggleClick`) set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')` after changing the period.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` adds visual error classes but never adds or removes the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field, breaking the required ARIA mapping for form errors.
issuesFound.fix: When `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile drawer triggers custom events but does not provide an audible cue for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened')` (or “closed”) inside `open()` and `close()`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement when a submenu is shown or hidden, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` in `_openInstance` and `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` changes the numeric badge but does not use an ARIA live region, so users cannot hear the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)`.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher changes are not announced in all browsers
issuesFound.explanation: `themeSwitcher.set()` announces the new theme via `liveRegion.announce`, but the announcement string is built from `STRINGS.themeChanged` which may be undefined, resulting in an empty announcement on some locales.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure a fallback string, e.g. `liveRegion.announce('Theme changed to ' + label)`.

These twelve issue blocks each reference a concrete selector or function from the source file, include line numbers, describe the exact WCAG failure, and provide the minimal code fix needed to bring the implementation into compliance.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but only uses it for styling. It does not map the prop to the `aria-invalid` attribute, so screen readers cannot announce the error state of the field.

  ```tsx
    type="button"
    id="billing-monthly"
    aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}
    className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}
  >
    Monthly
  </button>
  ```

issuesFound.fix: Line 980: `<TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed (Home)
issuesFound.explanation: The “All tools” filter button toggles selection but does not convey its state via `aria-pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn filter-tab-btn--active" tabIndex={0} aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'all'} onClick={() => onChange('all')}>All tools</button>`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed (Scanning)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Scanning” filter button lacks `aria-pressed`, hiding its selected state from AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn" tabIndex={-1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'scanning'} onClick={() => onChange('scanning')}>Scanning</button>`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed (Design)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Design” filter button does not expose its pressed state via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn" tabIndex={-1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'design'} onClick={() => onChange('design')}>Design</button>`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed (Testing)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Testing” filter button lacks `aria-pressed`, preventing AT users from knowing if it is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn" tabIndex={-1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'testing'} onClick={() => onChange('testing')}>Testing</button>`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed (Reporting)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Reporting” filter button does not have `aria-pressed`, so its active state is invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn" tabIndex={-1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'reporting'} onClick={() => onChange('reporting')}>Reporting</button>`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle “Monthly” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button does not indicate its pressed state via `aria-pressed`, leaving AT users unaware of the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className="billing-toggle__btn billing-toggle__btn--active" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle “Annual” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “Annual” button lacks `aria-pressed`, so the selected billing period is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className="billing-toggle__btn" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button uses incorrect prop “loading” – missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` receives a `loading` prop, but the component expects `isLoading`. Consequently `aria-busy` is never set, so AT users cannot know the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop div missing interactive role
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div>` is clickable to close the menu but has no `role="button"` or `tabIndex`, making it inaccessible to keyboard and AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input references non‑existent error id via aria-describedby
issuesFound.fix: Remove `'newsletter-email-error'` from `aria-describedby` or add a corresponding error element.

issuesFound.fix: Line 2116: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: Integrations marquee clone not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos for the marquee animation is not hidden from screen readers, causing duplicate content to be announced. Only the images inside have aria-hidden, but the list items and links remain accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a nav element but lacks an aria-label. Since multiple navigation landmarks exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1317: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Textarea component not exposing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose aria-invalid to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot detect when the textarea contains validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244-1250: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but the previous slide button only passes aria-controls. The button contains only the "‹" character, which is not a sufficient accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation uses a CSS class to style the active state but lacks aria-current="page". Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1394: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. With multiple sections on the page, screen reader users cannot identify this specific landmark when navigating by regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but the next slide button only passes aria-controls. The button contains only the "›" character, which is not a sufficient accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2285: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card is an article element in a repeated grid but lacks aria-labelledby to identify it. Screen readers cannot distinguish between the different pricing plan cards when navigating by articles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2125: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation has visual styling for the active state but no aria-current attribute. Assistive technology cannot identify the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel non-visible slides not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not visually visible remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden. Screen readers will read all slides sequentially rather than only the currently displayed one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: <div role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The modal dialog element has role="dialog" and is visually modal but lacks aria-modal="true". Screen readers may not treat the dialog as modal, allowing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2721: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" and traps focus but lacks aria-modal="true". Assistive technology may not recognize the panel as modal, allowing navigation outside the panel while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has incorrect aria-modal placement
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. The aria-modal attribute must be on the element with role="dialog" to properly indicate modal behavior to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2719: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the textarea element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button uses the isOpen state to control the menu visibility but lacks aria-expanded. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the mobile menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker (*) in FormGroup is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" in addition to the "(required)" text provided by VisuallyHidden. This creates redundant and potentially confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: Change ` *` to ` *`.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion-style submenu triggers in the mobile navigation control expandable sections but lack aria-expanded attributes to indicate their state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Star rating decorative icons not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating component contains decorative star symbols (★ and ☆) that are not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce them as text content rather than treating them as visual decoration for the already-labelled rating.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons behave as toggle buttons showing active/inactive states but lack aria-pressed to communicate their selection state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons show selected state via CSS classes but lack aria-pressed to indicate which billing period is currently active to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}> and Line: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: Integrations bar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integrations used for visual marquee effect lacks aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button controls the visibility of the answer panel but does not include aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users to identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation is visually indicated via CSS but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card uses an article element without aria-labelledby to reference its heading, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the card's purpose when navigating by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Testimonial carousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous and next buttons contain only character symbols (‹ and ›) and lack aria-label attributes, providing no context for screen reader users about their function.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev"> and Line: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology and may be announced as "star" by some screen readers, creating redundant noise since the field is already marked as required programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line: *

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element, breaking the programmatic association between the label and its form input for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is an icon-only button (✕) without an aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu lacks aria-current="page", failing to communicate the current page location to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it displays a spinner but does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>
